─[1]-Project──
lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Docs Health Sweep ]─ ┐

Docs Health Sweep

Three-pass algorithm for documentation purification. Optimized for maximum efficiency by reducing content volume before verification, then polishing what remains.

Algorithm: Reduce, Correct, Polish

Pass 1: Structural Consolidation (Reduce)

Scan all pages for duplicate facts, verbose sections, and thin content. Consolidate before fact-checking so we only verify canonical versions, not duplicates.

  • Identify duplicate facts across pages
  • Cut filler and verbose explanations
  • Merge thin sections into parent pages where appropriate
  • Ensure one source of truth per fact — everything else links

Pass 2: Fact Verification (Correct)

Extract every verifiable claim from consolidated docs and verify against source code. Split into groups for parallel verification.

  • Group A: index.md, getting-started.md — install commands, project structure
  • Group B: how-it-works.md — hook behavior, ownership, capture flow, context
  • Group C: Guides (7 files) — commands, config, conventions, cross-repo, interaction, platform support, roadmaps
  • Group D: Examples (4 files) — templates, frontmatter, naming conventions

Per group: extract claims → verify against lore source + create-lore → fix discrepancies.

Pass 3: Convention Compliance (Polish)

Final quality sweep against docs convention. Every page held to standard.

  • No filler phrases
  • Exact names (file paths, function names, CLI commands)
  • Consistent terminology across all pages
  • Pages under 150 lines
  • One topic per page
  • No stale TODOs or placeholders

Results

Line count: 1,614 → 1,381 (-14.4%)

Pages: 14 → 15 (extracted hook-architecture.md from how-it-works.md)

All pages under 150 lines except how-it-works.md (207) — justified by 5 mermaid architecture diagrams that are the core reference.

Pass 1 Findings (Structural)

CategoryCount
Duplicate facts consolidated20
Verbose sections cut10
Thin content merged3
Pages over 150 lines1 (how-it-works.md at 377 → split)
Terminology inconsistencies fixed9

Key actions: extracted Hook Architecture to its own page, moved tuning/observability content to configuration.md, collapsed redundant sections across roadmaps-and-plans/plan/roadmap pages, cut sales copy from cross-repo-workflow.md.

Pass 2 Findings (Fact Verification)

426 claims extracted across 4 groups. Key errors found and fixed:

ErrorFix
Hook counts wrong (Claude 5→6, OpenCode 4→5)Convention-guard was undocumented
MCP tool lore_write_guard missing from docsAdded to feature matrix and Cursor section
.cursorrules listed but doesn’t existRemoved; framework uses .cursor/rules/lore-*.mdc
Module layout diagram had wrong dependency arrowsFixed capture-nudge→config, context-path-guide→banner
”Four lifecycle points” should be fivePostToolUseFailure is separate
Convention guard fires on Write|Edit, not just WriteFixed in conventions.md
Sync description too narrowAdded hooks/, lib/, scripts/, .opencode/, .cursor/hooks/
.mdc generation source incompleteChanged to “multiple sources”

Pass 3 Findings (Convention Compliance)

CategoryFixes
Filler phrases removed12
Vague references made precise8
Duplicate prose replaced with links9
Inconsistent arrows (-> → →)5
Default conventions converted to table1 (saved ~20 lines)
Marketing/editorial sentences deleted3

Runbook Assessment

This three-pass algorithm works well as a repeatable process:

  1. Reduce first — cutting duplicates before fact-checking avoids verifying content that gets deleted. This saved significant effort in Pass 2.
  2. Parallel extraction + verification — splitting docs into 4 groups with haiku extraction → sonnet verification scaled well. Each pipeline was independent.
  3. Polish last — convention sweep catches issues introduced during edits and provides a final consistency pass.

Model allocation worked: haiku for structured extraction (fast, cheap), sonnet for code verification and editorial judgment.

Recommended cadence: run after any major feature release that touches hooks, config, or platform behavior. The fact-check pass is the highest-value step — convention drift accumulates slowly but factual errors appear immediately after code changes.

PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
